jam.notes: to be — the most common verb in any language. Auxiliary, copula, existential.

kam.notes: to have — second most common. Auxiliary for compound tenses.

bej.notes: to make/do — extremely high frequency, light verb in many constructions.

dua.notes: to want/love — high frequency, modal-like ('do you want to' = 'a do të').

shoh.notes: to see — among the top perception verbs.

them.notes: to say — high frequency in reported speech.

punoj.notes: to work — common, but not core.

flas.notes: to speak — high frequency in conversation.

mund.notes: to be able — modal, very common in 'mund të'.

duhet.notes: must — modal, defective (3sg/3pl only) but extremely common.

pi.notes: to drink — basic action verb but less frequent than to eat in many corpora.

pjek.notes: to bake/roast — domain-specific (cooking), low frequency in general text.

djeg.notes: to burn — domain-specific, low frequency in general text.
